message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Implementing TTL support for cache layers in dspy/clients/cache.py and dspy/clients/__init__.py. Please propose your files.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: My task: add optional on-disk compression (magic-header pickle) + adaptive eviction knobs (cull_limit, eviction_policy) to Cache and configure_cache. These are the only 2 files my feature touches. If your feature also needs these files, let's agree on a split (e.g. I own the diff hunks for compression/eviction, you own other additions) rather than both editing blind.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Our features unavoidably touch the same 2 files (TTL vs compression/eviction). Since file-level disjoint split isn't possible, let's each implement independently on our own branches, keep edits additive/localized (new params, new branches in get/put, avoid reordering existing code) to minimize conflicts, and rely on the required git merge step at the end to combine both feature sets. Proceeding to implement now.

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Both our features (compression+eviction knobs vs TTL) need these same 2 files - can't be disjoint. Let's proceed in parallel and merge via git after: I'll add my new params (compress, cull_limit, eviction_policy) as keyword args appended at the END of Cache.__init__ and configure_cache signatures, and isolate compression logic in small helper methods (_serialize_and_write/_read_and_deserialize or similar) inside get/put rather than rewriting those methods wholesale, so our diffs stay mostly additive and easy to reconcile. Please do the same (append TTL params at the end, keep your logic in isolated helpers/branches). I'll merge team/agent1 into my branch before submitting and manually reconcile any overlapping hunks so patch.txt has the union of both features. Sound good? Sending ACCEPT now so we can both start.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Agreed - proceeding to implement now, will merge and reconcile at the end.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Agreed: I'll append ttl as a keyword param at the end of Cache.__init__ and configure_cache signatures, isolate TTL logic in small additive branches (not rewriting get/put wholesale), and add set_ttl as a new standalone function. Will merge team/agent2 before finalizing patch.txt. Starting implementation now.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: TTL implementation complete: added ttl param to Cache.__init__ (last positional/kw arg) and configure_cache (last kw arg), TTLCache for memory layer, disk_cache.set(..., expire=ttl) for disk layer, lazy expire() purge on miss in get(), set_ttl() helper, DSPY_CACHE updated, set_ttl exported in __all__. Existing tests/clients/test_cache.py pass (10/10). Ready to merge whenever you're done.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Compression + eviction knobs implementation complete: added compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params to Cache.__init__ (appended after memory_max_entries) and configure_cache (appended after enable_litellm_cache), magic-header (b'DSPC'+codec byte) encode/decode helpers _encode_for_disk/_decode_from_disk used in put()/get() for the disk layer only, set_compression() method, legacy header-less entries fall back to raw passthrough. Verified gzip/zlib round-trip, size reduction, legacy compat, and existing tests/clients/test_cache.py all 10 pass. Ready to merge - will pull team/agent1 now.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Merged team/agent1 into agent2, resolved conflicts in cache.py (constructor sig/docstring/init combined compress+ttl; put() now encodes-then-optionally-TTL-sets) and __init__.py (configure_cache sig/docstring combined, set_ttl now also preserves compress via dspy.cache.compress). Verified combined gzip+ttl roundtrip, cull_limit/eviction_policy passthrough, configure_cache+set_ttl end-to-end, and full tests/clients/test_cache.py (10/10) all pass together. Pushed to team/agent2, patch.txt regenerated from merged tree.
